text: 胰岛素泵是怎么工作的？会随时监测血糖吗？
dear gpt, where is go the continuous body parameters sensors such as continuous glucose monitoring?

Most fasting and postprandial glucoses remain above goal ranges but improved since the last visit. Most of the blood glucose readings are between 130-170's and only two readings are above 200. To have a better picture of the variation in blood glucose reading CGM can be considered if patient glucose level is not improved. Patient's symptoms of hypoglycemia at normal blood glucose level may have been caused as patient's body is used to the high glucose level. Those symptoms may reduce when the blood glucose level is better controlled. Can increase the dose of Jardiance from 10 mg to 25 mg to better.

6. When a patient with diabetes displays signs of hypoglycemia and the point-of-care meter result indicates that they have a normal glucose, what should you do? Request an order for a metabolic panel. Administer sliding scale insulin per orders. Hold the patient’s meal tray pending test results. Repeat the point-of-care test. 7. What should be included in the individualized treatment plant when insulin is prescribed? Meal planning, continuous glucose monitoring Stress management, nutritional supplements Exercise, oral biguanides Meal planning, stress management, exercise 8. Once started on insulin, an inpatient's glycemic target will likely be between what amount? 110 mg/dL and 140 mg/dL 140 mg/dL and 180 mg/dL 80 mg/dL and 110 mg/dL 150 mg/dL and 170 mg/dL 9. What medication is ALWAYS needed for treatment of Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (T1DM?) Pioglitazone (Actos®) Amylin Insulin Repaglinide (Prandin®) 10. Which type of insulin coverage is used to cover the carbohydrate content of a meal? Sliding scale Correctional Prandial Basal
A continuous glucose monitoring system captures which type of data? Observational data Secondary data Simulation data Derived data
